title: Iran's radicalized regime fuels threat of nuclear bomb
subhead: Donald Trump seems to be considering ending the war with Tehran without addressing the nuclear issue. Yet the conflict has only made Iran's nuclear program more opaque and fueled the determination of its new leaders to acquire nuclear weapons.

Ten years after the Panama Papers, major advances in the fight against tax evasion
subhead: On April 3, 2016, the International Consortium of Investigative Journalists exposed the realities of offshore finance with a joint investigation with more than 100 media outlets, including Le Monde. It helped strengthen financial transparency and the fight against major international tax evasion.

title: Paris Mayor Grégoire unveils plan to tackle sexual violence in after-school programs: 'I want to guarantee complete transparency for families'
subhead: Paris's new mayor outlines his measures, including transparency, enhanced training and stricter oversight. Starting in April, a citizens' convention will debate the organization of the school week and day, including the current 4.5-day week for children.

title: French and South Korean presidents say will work together on Strait of Hormuz
subhead: On a visit to South Korea, French President Emmanuel Macron told his counterpart Lee Jae Myung that the two countries can play a role in helping to stabilize the situation in the Middle East, including the Strait of Hormuz, according to South Korean media.

title: Five Paris design exhibitions not to miss this spring
subhead: From Elizabeth Garouste's floral furniture to Gaetano Pesce's office pieces and Jean-Michel Wilmotte's sculptural works, this is Le Monde's selection of the French capital's must-see events this season.

'Refusing to divide French society into the racialized and the non-racialized does not mean denying the existence of racism'
subhead: Sociologist Stéphane Beaud and historian Gérard Noiriel analyze why people who have experienced racism – such as Bally Bagayoko, Saint-Denis's new mayor – may reject the label 'racialized.'

title: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship are now available online
subhead: From March 30, over 180,000 records are accessible on the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons' website, due to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of people's 40 years of exile in France.

title: Mystery among the gorillas: Who killed Dian Fossey?
subhead: More than 40 years after the murder of the primatologist at her camp in Rwanda, the existence of a piece of evidence, possibly kept in a laboratory of the French police, could revive the investigation.
